content:
So, is the little star moving to your desired account, but then when you reopen Thunderbird, it's back to the Gmail account? And the "Make Default" button's grayed-out property reverts as well?

Do you notice any other preferences not sticking? I ask because the Default Account designation is stored in the prefs.js file, located in your Profile. If one preference is not sticking, I would expect to see other properties not sticking, either.

First thing to try is to restart Thunderbird in Troubleshooting Mode. Go to menu item <Help | Troubleshoot Mode...>. Let it temporarily, not permanently, disable all your add-ons. After it restarts, try changing the default account again. If it's successful, that could indicate a misbehaving add-on.

Please let us know the results. If that doesn't do it there are some other things to try.
creator: rednoise

content:
I assume you're creating an HTML format message, rather than plain text.

Look at the Styles drop-down list in the Write Message window toolbar. "Paragraph" style gives you what looks like double-spacing, though what it really is a space between blocks of text that end with a carriage return. "Body Text" style makes successive blocks of text appear right next to each other without a gap.

This can be changed on the fly. You can set the default in <Settings | Composition | HTML Style> and enabling/disabling the "Use Paragraph format instead of Body Text by default" option as you like.

I may not understand your question, but I'll interpret it as best I can: you want to filter the message list to see only those that have Tags, as are defined in <Settings | General | Tags>?

content:
i will assume this is an IMAP account. If not,let me know, as steps would be different.

You can export your IMAP messages with these steps:

  1. verify that the account can be downloaded at 'synchronization&storage' pane and click 'advanced' tab
  2. then, check that you have 'sync regardless of age' at same pane
  3. next, click file>offline>download to ensure you get all content of all folders
  4. next, click help>troubleshootinginformation
  5. scroll to 'profile folder and click 'open folder'
  6. EXIt thunderbird. you are now in the profile
  7. click the imapmail folder and then the IMAP account folder
  8. Look for the message folders, identified by the combination names such as inbox, inbox.msf, sent, sent.msf, etc. IGNORE the msf entries, but COPY all of the others elsewhere
  9. start thunderbird
  10. install the 'importexport NG addon
  11. highlight Local Folders
  12. righclick and select the importexport option to import mbox
  13. locate and select one of the exported message folders, such as 'inbox'
  14. repeat the prior two steps for each exported message folder
  15. you should now see all account folders in Local Folders with their content. Check that content is there.
  16. You now have saved all account messages locally and, once you confirm to yourself that you have everything, you can remove that account.
  17. after setting up a new account, you are ready to COPY the saved messages to the new account.

After new IMAP account is set up, do the following:

  1. Create the desired folders in the new account
  2. For each folder, select messages in the Local Folders folder, and select the 'COPY to' option. IMAP was not designed for high-volume copying, so you may need to do that in batches. Or create a manual filter to do the COPY. Repeat for each folder. CHECK to ensure all messages are successfully copied.
  3. once satisfied, you may delete the Local Folders folders.

content:
Here are the steps:

  1. rightclick on newsgroup name in folder pane
  2. select settings
  3. select the 'synchronization&storange pane
  4. click the advanced tab and tick the box to allow downloading messages
  5. now, click File>offline>download and tick 'newsgroups
  6. now, your search should work and offer the 'body' option in search menu.
creator: davidsk

content:
david,

That worked for control-shift-K (but not control-K or control-shift-F), thank you, but I don’t really know what I have done.

“rightclick on newsgroup name in folder pane” – Yes.

“select settings” – I’m offered ‘Properties’, I hope that will do.

“select the 'synchronization&storange pane” I’m offered ‘Synchronisation’, I hope that will do.

“click the advanced tab and tick the box to allow downloading messages” There is no Advanced tab but there is a check box for 'Select this newsgroup for offline use', I selected it. There is also a button labelled 'Download Now', I pressed it.

“now, click File>offline>download and tick 'newsgroups” I went to File > Offline > Download/Sync Now and selected download and/or synchronise newsgroups, then clicked OK.

Do I enable 'Select this newsgroup for offline use' for each newsgroup I might want to search and then run ‘File > Offline > Download/Sync Now’ before each search?

Alan,

content: Yes, at the 'select for offline use', there should be a tick box for each newsgroup. I am not positive on whether file>offline>download is needed prior to serching, but I know it works. You might try searching at a later time after new messages are posted to see if a search is successful. My perception (and it's just that) is that the file>offline>download is needed. Good luck! :)
